title: cc-myasl config
description: Structured layout config for cc-myasl. One-flex-per-line is enforced at runtime by Config::validate_and_clamp, not statically by this schema. Note: this schema may be slightly stricter than the Rust runtime for segment disambiguation — the runtime uses #[serde(untagged)] and accepts a single segment object with both 'template' and 'flex' keys (silently resolving to Template); this schema uses oneOf and requires choosing exactly one variant.

$schema.description: URI of this JSON Schema; used by IDEs for auto-complete and validation.

lines.description: Ordered list of statusline rows (at most 3). Each line is rendered independently and joined with a newline.

powerline.description: When true, render segments as Powerline blocks with chevron transitions between adjacent background colors. Requires a Nerd Font for the chevron glyph.

Line.description: A single statusline row containing an ordered list of segments.

separator.description: Inserted between every pair of visible segments on this line. Defaults to an empty string.

segments.description: Ordered list of segments to render on this line.

Segment.description: Either a template segment (renders a format string) or a flex spacer (fills remaining terminal width).

TemplateSegment.description: A segment that renders a format-string template. Supports placeholder substitution (e.g. {model}, {five_left}).

template.description: Format string with zero or more {placeholder} tokens. Rendered text replaces the placeholders at runtime.

padding.description: Number of space characters added on each side of the rendered text (0–8). Clamped to 8 at runtime if exceeded.

hide_when_absent.description: When true, the segment is omitted entirely (including its adjacent separator) if every placeholder in the template resolves to an absent value.

color.description: Foreground (text) color using a named ANSI-16 color. Omit or set to null for the terminal default.

bg.description: Background color using a named ANSI-16 color. Omit or set to null for the terminal default.

FlexSegment.description: A spacer segment that expands to fill the remaining terminal width on this line. At most one flex segment per line is allowed (enforced at runtime).

flex.description: Must be true. A value of false is rejected by runtime validation.
